stories.title: John Deere Classic Round 2 Live — Davis Riley's Ace Headlines R1

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Davis Riley just made his first career PGA Tour hole-in-one at the John Deere Classic. Zach Johnson is at -7 and tied for third. Round 2 is underway at TPC Deere Run. #PGATour #JohnDeereClassic

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Amateur Preston Stout is in the hunt at the John Deere Classic. Viktor Hovland also in the field looking for back-to-back wins. It's a better field than usual this week. #PGATour #JohnDeereClassic

facebook.long_form:
Davis Riley etched his name in PGA Tour history on Thursday, making his first career hole-in-one during Round 1 of the 2026 John Deere Classic at TPC Deere Run in Silvis, Illinois.

Riley's ace added a memorable highlight to a first round that also featured Zach Johnson firing a 7-under 64 to land tied for third place. Johnson, a two-time major champion beloved in the Midwest, always draws extra energy when he competes at the John Deere — and he delivered in Round 1.

Amateur Preston Stout was also in contention after the opening round, giving golf fans a true amateur-against-the-world storyline to track. Two players are tied atop the leaderboard heading into Round 2.

The field this week is stronger than typical for a John Deere week, with Viktor Hovland, Jordan Spieth, Rickie Fowler, Sungjae Im, Ben Griffin, and Chris Gotterup all in the mix. Round 2 is underway today — the leaderboard is wide open.

#PGATour #JohnDeereClassic #Golf
facebook.image_caption: Davis Riley etched his name in PGA Tour history on Thursday, making his first career hole-in-one during Round 1 of the 2026 John Deere Classic at TPC Deere Run in Silvis, Illinois.

article.headline: John Deere Classic Round 2 Live: Davis Riley's Hole-in-One Steals the Show

stories.title: Open Championship Countdown — Royal Birkdale Ready in 13 Days

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: 13 days until the 154th Open Championship at Royal Birkdale. Over 1 million ticket applications. R&A projects attendance topping 300,000 — potentially an all-time record. #TheOpen #RoyalBirkdale

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: James Nicholas shot 65-67 at Burnham & Berrow to earn his first Open berth. Wesley Bryan missed a 7-footer on the 72nd hole and missed by one shot. Final Qualifying stories hit different. #TheOpen

facebook.long_form:
Thirteen days. That is all that separates the golf world from the 154th Open Championship at Royal Birkdale in Southport, England.

The storied links course is hosting The Open for the 11th time, and the R&A is projecting record-breaking attendance — CEO Mark Darbon expects more than 300,000 spectators for the week, potentially an all-time record. Over 1 million ticket applications were received.

Final Qualifying wrapped up on June 30 at four sites, with 20 spots up for grabs. James Nicholas stole the show at Burnham & Berrow, carding rounds of 65-67 (-10) to earn the medalist honors and, more importantly, his first-ever Open Championship berth. At West Lancashire, Wesley Bryan came agonizingly close — a missed 7-foot par putt on the 72nd hole cost him a spot by just one shot.

One final opportunity remains: a Last Chance Qualifier on Monday, July 13 at Royal Birkdale, with 12 players competing for a single coveted spot in the 156-man field.

Phil Mickelson has confirmed he will not be at Royal Birkdale. Tiger Woods, when asked about his return to competition, said only that "there is still some time" — leaving his Birkdale status unresolved.

The countdown is on. #TheOpen #RoyalBirkdale #Golf

article.headline: Open Championship Countdown: Royal Birkdale Ready in 13 Days

stories.title: Viktor Hovland Enters Deere as Travelers Champion — Can He Go Back-to-Back?

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text: Viktor Hovland won the Travelers Championship at -21, earned $3.6M and 700 FedExCup points. Now he's in the John Deere field. Can he go back-to-back? #PGATour #JohnDeereClassic

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text: Hovland's mom Galina watched from behind the 18th green at Travelers — his first win with her there. Then he beat Scheffler in a playoff. 8th career PGA Tour win. What a moment. #PGATour

facebook.long_form:
Viktor Hovland arrived at the John Deere Classic this week as one of the hottest players on the planet — and with a story that goes well beyond scorecards.

On June 29, Hovland claimed the Travelers Championship at TPC River Highlands in a Monday playoff after Sunday's round was suspended by fading daylight. His final score of 21-under par (rounds of 65-61-64-69) was enough to outlast Scottie Scheffler, who had made a clutch 8-foot par putt in near-darkness just to force the extra hole — before missing a 4-foot birdie putt on the first playoff hole to hand Hovland the trophy.

It was the Norwegian star's 8th career PGA Tour victory and his first since winning the 2025 Valspar Championship. He earned $3.6 million and 700 valuable FedExCup points.

But perhaps the most meaningful part of the week for Hovland was personal. His mother, Galina, was watching from behind the 18th green — making this the first PGA Tour victory she had witnessed in person. That alone made the win unforgettable.

Now Hovland is at TPC Deere Run with momentum and a chance to do something no player has done recently: win back-to-back on the Travelers-to-Deere stretch. History says it is difficult. Hovland's current form says he might not care.

#PGATour #JohnDeereClassic #Golf
facebook.image_caption: Viktor Hovland arrived at the John Deere Classic this week as one of the hottest players on the planet — and with a story that goes well beyond scorecards.

article.headline: Viktor Hovland Enters John Deere as Travelers Champion — Can He Go Back-to-Back?

stories.title: Scottie Scheffler Leads FedExCup Despite Travelers Heartbreak

x_posts.label: Post 4-1
x_posts.text: Scottie Scheffler leads the FedExCup with 3,511 points despite losing the Travelers playoff to Hovland. He made an 8-foot par putt in near-darkness just to get there. Still No. 1. #PGATour #FedExCup

x_posts.label: Post 4-2
x_posts.text: 2026 FedExCup top 5: Scheffler, McIlroy, JJ Spaun, Justin Rose, Tommy Fleetwood. The John Deere winner this week makes a big move up those standings. #PGATour #FedExCup #JohnDeereClassic

facebook.long_form:
Scottie Scheffler's 2026 season has been defined by excellence — and lately, by heartbreak at the finish line. After making a clutch 8-foot par putt in near-darkness to force a playoff at the Travelers Championship, Scheffler then missed a 4-footer for birdie on the first extra hole and watched Viktor Hovland claim the trophy.

And yet, even after that gut-punch, Scheffler remains the No. 1 player in the 2026 FedExCup standings with 3,511 points.

Here is where things stand heading into the John Deere Classic:

1. Scottie Scheffler — 3,511 pts
2. Rory McIlroy
3. JJ Spaun
4. Justin Rose
5. Tommy Fleetwood
6. Ben Griffin
7. Russell Henley
8. Sepp Straka
9. Robert MacIntyre
10. Maverick McNealy

Hovland's 700-point gain from the Travelers win has him surging up the standings as well. The John Deere winner this week will collect a significant points haul — especially important as many of the world's top players are sitting out this week ahead of The Open Championship (July 16–19 at Royal Birkdale).

For context, Scheffler also finished T4 at the 2026 U.S. Open, which was won by Wyndham Clark at Shinnecock Hills at -4. Scheffler has been relentlessly consistent — even when the trophies aren't coming.

#PGATour #FedExCup #Golf
facebook.image_caption: Scottie Scheffler's 2026 season has been defined by excellence — and lately, by heartbreak at the finish line.

article.headline: Scottie Scheffler Leads FedExCup Despite Travelers Heartbreak

stories.title: Jackson Koivun's Pro Debut Turns Heads at TPC Deere Run

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Jackson Koivun is making his official PGA Tour pro debut at the John Deere Classic this week. He made the cut at the U.S. Open as an amateur and has three top-6 Tour finishes. Watch this guy. #PGATour

article.headline: Jackson Koivun's Pro Debut Turns Heads at TPC Deere Run
